title: Police Arrest Male Following Weapons Incident in Kitchener
content:
Posted on:
Thursday June 11
Location:
Kitchener
Occurrence #:
WA26084802
Waterloo Regional Police have arrested a male following a weapons incident in Kitchener.
On June 11, 2026, at approximately 1:45 a.m., police responded to reports of a male brandishing a knife in the area of Monte Carlo Street.
Officers arrived and arrested the male. Through the investigation, it was determined that the male engaged in a physical altercation and brandished a knife towards the victim. The victim reported minor injuries.
A 24-year-old Kitchener male has been charged with the following:
Assault with a weapon
Uttering threats to cause death or bodily harm (three counts)
Possession of a weapon for a dangerous purpose
Mischief under $5,000
Assault peace officer
Breach of release order
The accused was held for a bail hearing.
Anyone with information is asked to contact police at 519-570-9777, extension 4496.
Anonymous information can be provided to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477 or online at
www.waterloocrimestoppers.com
.

title: Police Continue to Investigate Hate-Motivated Assault in Kitchener
content:
Posted on:
Thursday June 11
Location:
Kitchener
Occurrence #:
WA26084491
Waterloo Regional Police are investigating a hate-motivated assault in Kitchener.
On June 10, 2026, at approximately 12 p.m., a victim was assaulted by an unknown female in the area of Victoria Street North and Weber Street West. The suspect uttered racial slurs at the victim before fleeing the area.
The victim, a 57-year-old male reported no physical injuries.
The suspect was described as a white female, approximately 5’7”, wearing all black with a blue baseball cap.
The investigation remains ongoing.
Anyone with information is asked to contact the Waterloo Regional Police Service at 519-570-9777, extension 6399.
Anonymous information can be provided to Crime Stoppers by calling 1-800-222-8477 or by visiting
www.waterloocrimestoppers.com
.

title: Police Arrest and Charge Waterloo Male in Child Exploitation Investigation
content:
Posted on:
Wednesday June 10
Location:
Waterloo
Occurrence #:
WA26012311
Waterloo Regional Police have charged a Waterloo male as part of an investigation involving child sexual abuse and exploitation material.
In May 2026, WRPS’ Cybercrime-Internet Child Exploitation Unit conducted an investigation involving the luring of children, making child sexual abuse and exploitation material, and making sexually explicit material available to a child.
On June 10, 2026, investigators completed a search warrant at a residence in Waterloo. As a result of the investigation, 43-year-old Waterloo male was arrested and charged with:
Possession of Child Sexual Assault and Exploitation Material
Making Child Sexual Assault and Exploitation Material
Luring a Child with a Telecommunication Device
Making Sexually Explicit Material Available to a Child
The accused was held in custody for a bail hearing.
The Waterloo Regional Police Service is a member of the Ontario Provincial Strategy to Protect Children from Sexual Abuse and Exploitation on the Internet. This initiative is supported by a grant from the Ministry of the Solicitor General.

title: WRPS Lay Weapon-Related Charges Following Disturbance in Kitchener
content:
Posted on:
Tuesday June 9
Location:
Kitchener
Occurrence #:
WA26083754
Waterloo Regional Police have laid charges following a disturbance in Kitchener.
On June 9, 2026, at approximately 8:25 a.m., officers responded to the area of Kent Avenue and Charles Street East for reports of a disturbance.
An unknown male was observed yelling threatening remarks at members of the public while brandishing an edged weapon.
There were no reported physical injuries.
Police located the 35-year-old male from Waterloo in the area and placed him under arrest.
As a result of the investigation, the male has been charged with Possession of a Weapon for a Dangerous Purpose, Assault with a Weapon, and Uttering Threats to Cause Bodily Harm.
The accused was held in police custody for a bail hearing.
Anyone with information is asked to contact Waterloo Regional Police at 519-570-9777. Anonymous information can be provided to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477 or online at www.waterloocrimestoppers.com.

title: Waterloo Regional Police Investigating a Fatal Collision in Kitchener
content:
Posted on:
Monday June 8
Location:
Kitchener
Occurrence #:
WA26048735
UPDATED MEDIA RELEASE:
Waterloo Regional Police Service's Traffic Services Unit have laid charges in connection to a fatal motor vehicle collision in Kitchener.
On April 3, 2026, emergency services responded to a collision involving a GMC pickup truck and an e-bike at the intersection of Lancaster Street and Guelph Street in Kitchener.
The 44-year-old e-bike operator was transported to a local hospital, where he later succumbed to his injuries.
As a result of the investigation, a 38-year-old Kitchener male has been charged with Careless Driving Causing Death and Turn Not in Safety.
PREVIOUS MEDIA RELEASE:
Members of the Waterloo Regional Police Service's Traffic Services Unit are investigating a fatal motor vehicle collision in Kitchener.
On April 3, 2026, at approximately 9:45 p.m., emergency services responded to reports of a collision involving a GMC pickup truck and an e‑bike at the intersection of Lancaster Street and Guelph Street.
The driver of the e‑bike, a 44‑year‑old Kitchener male, was transported by paramedics to a local hospital, where he succumbed to his injuries. The driver of the GMC pickup truck, a 38‑year‑old Kitchener male, did not sustain any physical injuries.
The intersection was closed for several hours while police conducted the investigation.
The investigation is ongoing.
Anyone who witnessed the collision or has dash camera footage is encouraged to contact the Waterloo Regional Police Service at 519‑570‑9777. Anonymous tips can be provided to Crime Stoppers at 1‑800‑222‑8477 or online at
www.waterloocrimestoppers.com
.

title: Waterloo Regional Police Investigating Daytime Robbery in Cambridge
content:
Posted on:
Monday June 8
Location:
Cambridge
Occurrence #:
WA26082788
Waterloo Regional Police are investigating a robbery in Cambridge after an older adult was accosted by a group of suspects armed with a weapon.
On June 7, 2026, at approximately 12:45 p.m., police responded to reports of an assault in the area of Preston Parkway.
It was reported that a 78-year-old male was approached by three teenage males, one of whom brandished a weapon, and demanded personal property. When the victim refused, the three males assaulted him. The victim sustained a minor physical injury.
The suspects fled the area on foot prior to police arrival and were last seen heading southbound on Preston Parkway.
The suspects are described as White males, approximately 16 to 18 years old, all wearing t-shirts, with one wearing a red t-shirt.
The Waterloo Regional Police Service’s General Investigations Unit is continuing to investigate.
Anyone with information, including security camera footage, is encouraged to contact police at 519-570-9777, extension 4496. Anonymous information can be provided to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477 or online at
www.waterloocrimestoppers.com
.

title: Waterloo Regional Police Investigating Collision in Kitchener After Cyclist Injured
content:
Posted on:
Monday June 8
Location:
Kitchener
Occurrence #:
WA26082966
Waterloo Regional Police are investigating a collision in Kitchener after a cyclist was struck by a vehicle.
On June 7, 2026, at approximately 7:50 p.m., emergency services responded to reports of a collision at the intersection of Victoria Street North and Bruce Street involving a Honda motor vehicle and a bicycle.
The cyclist, a 56-year-old female from Kitchener, was transported to a local hospital with injuries. The driver of the Honda, a 66-year-old female from Wellington County, was not physically injured.
The intersection was partially closed for several hours while officers conducted the investigation.
The investigation remains ongoing by WRPS’ Traffic Services Unit, and charges are anticipated.
Anyone who witnessed the collision or has dash camera footage is encouraged to call police at 519-570-9777, extension 8856. Anonymous information can be provided to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477 or online at
www.waterloocrimestoppers.com
.

title: Waterloo Regional Police Seeking to Confirm Well-Being Following Suspicious Incident in Kitchener
content:
Posted on:
Sunday June 7
Location:
Kitchener
Occurrence #:
WA26082736
UPDATED MEDIA RELEASE:
Waterloo Regional Police Service's General Investigations Unit has identified the individuals and vehicle involved in a suspicious person report in Kitchener.
On June 7, 2026, police received a report of a suspicious incident at the Walter Bean Grand River Trail parking lot on King Street East.
Investigators subsequently identified the two involved individuals and the vehicle. The incident is not considered suspicious.
PREVIOUS MEDIA RELEASE:
Waterloo Regional Police are investigating a suspicious incident in Kitchener and are seeking to confirm the well-being of those involved.
On June 7, 2026, at approximately 10:40 a.m., a witness observed a concerning interaction in the parking lot of a natural area located at 3595 King Street East. A male was seen escorting a female into a vehicle after the female had been observed climbing a hill near a pedestrian bridge.
The suspect vehicle is believed to be a black 2021–2023 Kia Sorento displaying a Nova Scotia licence plate. It was last seen travelling westbound on King Street East toward River Road East. Photographs of the vehicle were obtained by police; however, attempts to locate the vehicle or confirm the licence plate have been unsuccessful at this time.
[photos removed; vehicle and involved parties identified]
Suspect Description:
White male
Approximately 40 years old
Thin build
Short grey hair, nearly bald
Slight beard
Wearing a white shirt, blue jeans, and black shoes
Female Description:
White female
Approximately mid-20s to 30s
Orange/blonde hair (appears previously dyed red and partially bleached)
Wearing a burgundy sweater and black leggings
The investigation is ongoing, and police are looking to confirm the well-being of the individuals involved.
Anyone with information is asked to contact the Waterloo Regional Police Service Tip Line at 519-570-9777, extension 4496. Anonymous information can be provided to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477 or online at www.waterloocrimestoppers.com.

title: Waterloo Regional Police Investigating Hate-Motivated Incident in Kitchener
content:
Posted on:
Sunday June 7
Location:
Kitchener
Occurrence #:
WA26082542
UPDATED MEDIA RELEASE:
Waterloo Regional Police have arrested a male in connection to a hate-motivated incident in Kitchener.
On June 6, 2026, at approximately 10:15 p.m., two individuals were in the area of Bankside Drive and Benesfort Drive when they were approached by an unknown male. The male became aggressive, yelled racial comments at the victims, and engaged in a physical altercation.
As a result of the investigation, on June 9, 2026, a 52-year-old Kitchener male was arrested and charged with:
Assault (two counts)
Mischief under $5,000
Mischief- interfere with lawful use or enjoyment
Dangerous operation of a vehicle
Anyone with information is asked to contact police at 519-570-9777, extension 4496.
Anonymous information can be provided to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477 or online at
www.waterloocrimestoppers.com
.

title: Waterloo Regional Police Investigating Weapons Incident in Kitchener
content:
Posted on:
Sunday June 7
Location:
Kitchener
Occurrence #:
WA26082622
Waterloo Regional Police are investigating a weapons-related incident in Kitchener.
On June 7, 2026, at approximately 1 a.m., three youths were in the area of Fairway Road South and Highway 8 South when they were approached by multiple individuals in a vehicle. A weapon was discharged, and the youths fled the area.
There were no reported physical injuries.
The suspect vehicle is described as a dark-coloured, older model sedan.
The investigation is ongoing.
Anyone with information is asked to contact the Waterloo Regional Police Service Tip Line at 519-570-9777, extension 4496. Anonymous information can be provided to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477 or online at
www.waterloocrimestoppers.com
.

title: WRPS Continue to Investigate Hate-Motivated Graffiti in Woolwich
content:
Posted on:
Friday June 12
Location:
Woolwich
Occurrence #:
WA26085302
Waterloo Regional Police are continuing to investigate a hate-motivated incident in Woolwich.
On June 11, 2026, at approximately 9:15 p.m., police received a report of graffiti at a residence in the area of Dolman Street.
The graffiti included hate-motivated writing.
The investigation is ongoing.
Anyone with information is encouraged to call police at 519-570-9777, extension 4499.
Anonymous tips can be provided to Crime Stoppers by calling 1-800-222-8477 or online at
www.waterloocrimestoppers.com
.
